description: Catherine joined HM Courts and Tribunals Service (HMCTS) as Chief Financial Officer in June 2024.  Prior to that Catherine was Finance and Business Director for Transformation/Change in HMRC. This isn’t Catherine’s first role in HMCTS: she was previously responsible for Strategic Finance and Planning where she notably played a key role in our Covid-19 response and recovery efforts.  Her wider experience includes leading a £3bn delivery programme across England whilst at the Department for Work and Pensions, a number of years working at the European Commission in Brussels, and a range of policy and analytical roles in the earlier parts of her UK civil service career. Catherine is a chartered management accountant, holds a PhD, and is also a qualified executive coach.  Catherine is passionate about getting strategic transformation right: she believes in the strength of multi-disciplinary teams, where enabling functions are at the heart of decision-making.

details.body:
Catherine joined HM Courts and Tribunals Service (HMCTS) as Chief Financial Officer in June 2024.  Prior to that Catherine was Finance and Business Director for Transformation/Change in HMRC.

This isn’t Catherine’s first role in HMCTS: she was previously responsible for Strategic Finance and Planning where she notably played a key role in our Covid-19 response and recovery efforts.  Her wider experience includes leading a £3bn delivery programme across England whilst at the Department for Work and Pensions, a number of years working at the European Commission in Brussels, and a range of policy and analytical roles in the earlier parts of her UK civil service career.

Catherine is a chartered management accountant, holds a PhD, and is also a qualified executive coach.  Catherine is passionate about getting strategic transformation right: she believes in the strength of multi-disciplinary teams, where enabling functions are at the heart of decision-making.

Catherine joined HM Courts and Tribunals Service (HMCTS) as Chief Financial Officer in June 2024.  Prior to that Catherine was Finance and Business Director for Transformation/Change in HMRC.

This isn’t Catherine’s first role in HMCTS: she was previously responsible for Strategic Finance and Planning where she notably played a key role in our Covid-19 response and recovery efforts.  Her wider experience includes leading a £3bn delivery programme across England whilst at the Department for Work and Pensions, a number of years working at the European Commission in Brussels, and a range of policy and analytical roles in the earlier parts of her UK civil service career.

Catherine is a chartered management accountant, holds a PhD, and is also a qualified executive coach.  Catherine is passionate about getting strategic transformation right: she believes in the strength of multi-disciplinary teams, where enabling functions are at the heart of decision-making.

The HMCTS Chief Finance Officer is responsible for:

  • financial plans for day-to-day operations
  • strategic objectives
  • financial management, governance and controls
  • property management

role.title: Chief Finance Officer and Board Member

role_appointments.title: Catherine Blair - Chief Finance Officer and Board Member
